matched_testcase_text: test case id: tc-registration-001 title: user successfully registers module: registration source pages: 2,3 preconditions: test data: - username: testuser - password: testpass123 - email: testuser@example.com test steps: 1. user navigates to the registration page. 2. user fills in the registration form with the provided test data. 3. user submits the form. 4. application sends a registration request to the server. 5. server processes the request and saves the user data. 6. server generates an otp and sends it to the user via email. 7. user receives the email and enters the otp. 8. server verifies the otp and completes the registration. expected result: user is successfully registered and redirected to the homepage.

matched_testcase_text: test case id: tc-chat-003 title: user successfully receives a message module: chat source pages: 8 preconditions: user is logged in and has a chat with another user test data: - message: hi there! test steps: 1. user2 navigates to the chat page. 2. user2 types the message "hi there!". 3. user2 sends the message to user1. 4. application sends a message request to the server. 5. server processes the request and saves the message in the database. 6. server sends the message to user1's application. 7. user1 receives the message notification. 8. user1 opens the chat and sees the message. expected result: user1 sees the message "hi there!" in the chat.

item_text: the diagram models the process of a user (utente1) sending a message to another user (utente2).
matched_testcase_text: test case id: tc-chat-001 title: user successfully sends a message module: chat source pages: 8 preconditions: user is logged in and has a chat with another user test data: - message: hello! test steps: 1. user1 navigates to the chat page. 2. user1 types the message "hello!". 3. user1 sends the message to user2. 4. application sends a message request to the server. 5. server processes the request and saves the message in the database. 6. server sends the message to user2's application. 7. user2 receives the message notification. 8. user2 opens the chat and sees the message. expected result: user2 sees the message "hello!" in the chat.
